chain_fix_notes: DAS-VERIFY-0 verifier now validates both stored prev_digest field against tracked chain position AND computed[:24] hash against stored record_hash. Two independent tamper signals. Fixes the off-by-n suffix comparison from prior session.
summary: World-first hermetic, deterministic CEL epoch sandbox with cryptographically verifiable JSONL audit ledger. DAS enables any external observer to clone the ADAAD repository, execute docker-compose up das-demo, and receive a chain-verified 8-record epoch ledger in under 60 seconds — no credentials, no cloud infrastructure. The HMAC-SHA256 chain uses canonical payload json.dumps({prev, sub}, sort_keys=True) with 24-char prefix storage. DAS-VERIFY-0 detects tampering at two levels: stored prev_digest vs tracked chain position, and stored record_hash vs re-derived computed prefix. DAS-DOCKER-0 constitutionally prohibits :latest Docker image tags, enforcing exact digest pinning at invariant level. DAS-REPLAY-0 guarantees identical record_hash re-derivation from stored JSONL on every replay. DAS-DETERM-0 gates all timestamp generation through RuntimeDeterminismProvider. DAS-GATE-0 enforces fail-closed exit codes across all three CLI scripts.
ip_claims: Hermetic, deterministic CEL epoch sandbox with HMAC-SHA256 JSONL chain-linkage enabling external auditability of constitutionally governed autonomous AI mutations without live system access
DAS-DOCKER-0: Docker :latest tag prohibition enforced as a Hard-class constitutional invariant in an autonomous AI governance system

Dual-signal tamper detection in verify_ledger(): independent validation of stored prev_digest field and re-derived HMAC hash prefix, providing two cryptographically independent tamper indicators per record
RuntimeDeterminismProvider as constitutional timestamp oracle: DAS-DETERM-0 prohibits datetime.now(), time.time(), and uuid4() in sandbox epoch execution, enforced at module level
world_first_claim: First autonomous AI governance system to offer a hermetic, publicly runnable demo sandbox producing a cryptographically chain-verified mutation audit ledger from a single docker-compose command, with constitutional prohibition on non-deterministic timestamps and :latest Docker image tags
